a:T284e,<div class="specification">
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">Liquid-crystal displays (LCDs) have many uses.</span></p>
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">A molecule which acts as a thermotropic liquid crystal is shown.</span></p>
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;"><img height="122" src="data:image/png;base64," style="margin-right: auto; margin-left: auto; display: block;" width="400"/></span></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">State the name of the functional group which allows the molecule to be responsive to applied electric fields.</span></p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">Explain the effects of very low and high temperatures on the liquid-crystal behaviour of this molecule.</span></p>
<p> </p>
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">Low temperature: </span></p>
<p><span style="background-color: #ffffff;">High temperature:</span></p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>

<strong>[✔]</strong></span></p>\n<p><span style=\"background-color: #ffffff;\"><em><strong>Note</strong>: Accept “cyano\".</em></span></p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p><span style=\"background-color: #ffffff;\"><em>Low temperature</em>: <br/>intermolecular forces prevent molecules moving AND solid/«normal» crystal formation  <strong>[✔]</strong></span></p>\n<p><span style=\"background-color: #ffffff;\"><em>High temperature</em>:<br/>«above a critical temperature» disrupts alignment of molecules AND <strong>AND</strong> behaves as fluid/liquid”.</span></em></p>\n<div for the polarity of the liquid crystal molecule.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Very poorly answered with candidates
